name: AXR: Agent eXecution Readiness
description: A 100-point rubric measuring how safely and productively a software repository can be operated by coding agents.

0.label: Absent
0.description: Nothing in place. Agents operate blind.

1.label: Ad hoc
1.description: Exists but inconsistent, stale, undocumented, or unreliable.

2.label: Functional
2.description: Works, but agents need human scaffolding to use it reliably.

3.label: Strong
3.description: Agents can use it autonomously in most normal cases.

4.label: Exemplary
4.description: Intentionally designed for agentic consumption; self-reinforcing, discoverable, and safe.

score_bands.label: Agent-Native
score_bands.description: Agents can drive meaningful work with light supervision.

score_bands.label: Agent-Ready
score_bands.description: Productive with targeted human review at defined checkpoints.

score_bands.label: Agent-Assisted
score_bands.description: Useful for scoped tasks; humans still steer architecture and validation.

score_bands.label: Agent-Hazardous
score_bands.description: Agents likely create more cleanup than value.

score_bands.label: Agent-Hostile
score_bands.description: Fix foundations before enabling agentic workflows.

dimensions.name: Tests & CI Signal
dimensions.question: Can the agent trust feedback when it changes code?

criteria.name: Deterministic test suite under 10 minutes
criteria.description: Test suite runs deterministically in under 10 minutes for common local and CI paths.

criteria.name: Meaningful coverage at module boundaries
criteria.description: Coverage is meaningful at module boundaries and critical workflows, not vanity percentage.
evidence_hints: Presence of integration/workflow tests vs unit-only
Test file organization relative to module structure

criteria.name: Flaky tests tracked and quarantined
criteria.description: Flaky tests are explicitly tracked and quarantined, not silently tolerated.
evidence_hints: Flaky test tracking issues or labels

criteria.name: CI failures map to actionable messages
criteria.description: CI failures produce precise, actionable error messages rather than opaque output.
evidence_hints: Recent failed CI runs via gh run view

criteria.name: Fast-fail pre-commit/pre-push checks
criteria.description: Fast-fail checks exist pre-commit or pre-push for lint, format, type, and obvious breakage.

dimensions.name: Docs & Agent Context
dimensions.question: Does the repo explain itself to a cold-start agent?

criteria.name: Root CLAUDE.md or AGENTS.md
criteria.description: A root-level CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or equivalent explains architecture, conventions, sharp edges, and operating rules.
evidence_hints: CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or .claude/CLAUDE.md at repo root
Presence of architecture, conventions, gotchas sections

criteria.name: README covers setup in ≤5 commands
criteria.description: README covers setup, run, test, and deploy in five commands or fewer for common workflows.
evidence_hints: Presence of Quickstart, Setup, or Getting Started section

criteria.name: Local READMEs for non-obvious subsystems
criteria.description: Non-obvious subsystems have local documentation or module READMEs.
evidence_hints: Ratio of documented to undocumented modules

criteria.name: ADRs or decision log
criteria.description: ADRs or decision logs explain why important patterns, tradeoffs, and constraints exist.
evidence_hints: Dated entries with context and consequences

criteria.name: Domain glossary
criteria.description: Domain glossary exists for critical business language, especially in regulated or specialized domains.
evidence_hints: Domain terms defined in CLAUDE.md or README

dimensions.name: Change Surface Clarity
dimensions.question: Can the agent find the right place to change and understand what the change will affect?

criteria.name: Business logic locatable by responsibility
criteria.description: Business logic is easy to locate by responsibility, not hidden across unrelated files.
evidence_hints: Directory structure aligned with domain concepts
Absence of god files or misc/utils dumping grounds

criteria.name: Module boundaries and public interfaces explicit
criteria.description: Module boundaries and public interfaces are explicit and discoverable.

criteria.name: Integration points and contracts documented
criteria.description: Integration points, schemas, contracts, and event boundaries are documented or enforced.

criteria.name: Examples and reference implementations for key workflows
criteria.description: Examples, fixtures, or reference implementations exist for key workflows.

criteria.name: Context packing support
criteria.description: Repository supports generating bounded context maps (repo tree, module summaries, repomix/llm-tree output) that fit standard agent windows without overflow.
evidence_hints: llm-tree, code2prompt, or similar tool config
Scripts that generate repo context bundles
Per-module summary files or generated indexes

dimensions.name: Safety Rails
dimensions.question: What stops the agent when it is wrong?

criteria.name: HITL checkpoints on destructive operations
criteria.description: Human-in-the-loop checkpoints exist for destructive or high-risk operations: migrations, production writes, external API side effects, irreversible actions.

criteria.name: Reversible by default
criteria.description: Operations are reversible by default where practical: migrations, deploys, data changes, bulk updates.

criteria.name: Secrets not in repo
criteria.description: Secrets are never stored in-repo, and access is scoped through environment or vault controls.
evidence_hints: Vault, GCP Secret Manager, or equivalent references

criteria.name: Branch protection on main
criteria.description: Branch protection and required review guard critical branches.

criteria.name: Agent boundaries documented
criteria.description: Agent permissions and operational boundaries are explicitly documented, including what it may and may not touch.

dimensions.name: Legibility
dimensions.question: Can an agent read and navigate the codebase without human explanation?

criteria.name: Typical change fits one context window
criteria.description: Source files have low median LOC, reducing context window overflow risk for agent-driven changes.

criteria.name: Tiered context beyond root README
criteria.description: Agent-specific context tooling exists beyond the root README — context maps, repomix configs, or per-module agent instructions.

criteria.name: Agent instructions don't contradict
criteria.description: Multiple instruction files (CLAUDE.md, .cursorrules, AGENTS.md) are cross-referenced or consolidated. Single unified file scores higher than fragmented.

criteria.name: Conventions enforced not just documented
criteria.description: Conventions documented in CLAUDE.md are backed by matching lint rules or CI checks.
evidence_hints: Lint config files matching documented conventions

criteria.name: Non-obvious decisions documented
criteria.description: ADRs and inline comments cover architectural choices; commit messages explain 'why' not just 'what'.

dimensions.name: Patterns
dimensions.question: Does the codebase use one way to do each thing?

criteria.name: Duplication scanning configured
criteria.description: Code duplication detection tools (jscpd, CPD, SonarQube duplication) are configured locally or in CI.

criteria.name: Single approach per concern
criteria.description: Each cross-cutting concern (error handling, logging, HTTP clients, config loading) uses one consistent approach.

criteria.name: No competing patterns
criteria.description: The codebase does not contain competing frameworks, libraries, or architectural patterns doing the same job.

criteria.name: Shallow import depth
criteria.description: Import chains are shallow, reducing cognitive and context-window overhead.

criteria.name: Consistent error handling
criteria.description: Error handling follows a single pattern: consistent error types, propagation style, and user-facing messages.

dimensions.name: Style & Validation
dimensions.question: Is code quality enforced mechanically before review?

criteria.name: Type checker clean or baselined
criteria.description: Type checker runs clean or against an enforced baseline.

criteria.name: Linter and formatter in local and CI
criteria.description: Linter and formatter run consistently locally and in CI.

criteria.name: Formatting actively enforced
criteria.description: Formatting is actively enforced via pre-commit hooks and CI, not just configured.

criteria.name: Static analysis beyond linting
criteria.description: Static analysis tools beyond basic linting are configured and integrated.

criteria.name: Editor/IDE config shared
criteria.description: Editor and IDE configuration is shared in the repository for consistent developer experience.

dimensions.name: Structure & Modularity
dimensions.question: Can the agent change one thing without breaking ten?

criteria.name: Clear module boundaries and sane dependencies
criteria.description: Module boundaries are clear and dependency direction is sane.

criteria.name: No circular dependencies
criteria.description: Circular or backdoor dependencies are prevented or rare.
evidence_hints: pydeps or pylint circular import detection (Python)

criteria.name: Files scoped for local reasoning
criteria.description: Files and functions are scoped so changes can be understood without opening half the repo.

criteria.name: Consistent searchable naming
criteria.description: Naming conventions are consistent and searchable.

criteria.name: Dead code removed
criteria.description: Dead code is removed rather than left behind as misleading clutter.

dimensions.name: Tooling & Reproducibility
dimensions.question: Are the local feedback loops fast, reproducible, and unambiguous?

criteria.name: Reproducible hermetic build
criteria.description: Build/dev environment is reproducible and reasonably hermetic.

criteria.name: One-command local bootstrap
criteria.description: One-command local bootstrap exists (bin/setup, make dev, etc.).
evidence_hints: Makefile with dev or setup target

criteria.name: Pinned dependencies with upgrade path
criteria.description: Dependency versions are pinned, and upgrade paths or procedures are documented.

criteria.name: Dev container or codespace support
criteria.description: Development container or cloud codespace configuration exists for reproducible environments.

criteria.name: Build cache or incremental feedback
criteria.description: Build caching or incremental build tools are configured for fast feedback loops.
evidence_hints: CI cache steps (actions/cache with build patterns)

dimensions.name: Supply Chain
dimensions.question: Are dependencies managed, audited, and current?

criteria.name: Vulnerability scanning configured
criteria.description: Dependency vulnerability scanning is configured via audit tools or CI integration.

criteria.name: Lockfile verified in CI
criteria.description: CI installs from lockfile with frozen/locked flags to prevent drift.

criteria.name: Automated dependency upgrades
criteria.description: Renovate, Dependabot, or equivalent configured with auto-merge for safe updates.

criteria.name: Dependencies are fresh
criteria.description: Lockfiles are recently updated, indicating active dependency maintenance.
evidence_hints: Lockfile last-modified date via git log
Age in days of most recent lockfile update

criteria.name: Minimal dependency surface
criteria.description: Dependencies are justified — no redundant packages, abandoned libraries, or excessive dep count relative to project complexity.

dimensions.name: Execution Visibility
dimensions.question: Can the agent see what it did and what happened afterward?

criteria.name: Structured logging with consistent fields
criteria.description: Structured logging uses consistent fields and meaningful event names.

criteria.name: Agent-touchable paths expose telemetry
criteria.description: Agent-touchable code paths expose useful logs, traces, metrics, or artifacts.

criteria.name: Errors route to single searchable place
criteria.description: Errors route to a single searchable place.

criteria.name: Local dev preserves diagnostic output
criteria.description: Local development preserves enough output to diagnose failures without guessing.

criteria.name: Test failures preserve logs and artifacts
criteria.description: Test and run failures preserve logs, stack traces, and artifacts needed for root-cause analysis.

dimensions.name: Workflow Realism
dimensions.question: Can the agent safely rehearse real work in conditions that resemble production behavior?

criteria.name: Representative fixtures for core workflows
criteria.description: Representative fixtures, sample data, or synthetic records exist for core workflows.
evidence_hints: Sample inputs for core business flows

criteria.name: Sandbox mirrors production
criteria.description: Sandbox or dev flows mirror real operational paths closely enough to validate business behavior.

criteria.name: External integrations stubable
criteria.description: External integrations can be simulated or stubbed meaningfully.

criteria.name: Golden-path scenarios
criteria.description: Golden-path scenarios exist for critical user or business flows.

criteria.name: Regression artifacts for important outputs
criteria.description: Regression artifacts or before/after comparisons exist for important outputs.
